mission.title: external-secrets: PushSecrets should implement the same templating options as ExternalSecrets
mission.description: PushSecrets should implement the same templating options as ExternalSecrets. This issue affects 14+ users.

steps.title: Identify external-secrets feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your external-secrets deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=external-secrets
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: PushSecrets should implement the same templating options as ExternalSecrets

steps.title: Check current Secret configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant external-secrets resources:
```bash
kubectl get secret -A
kubectl describe secret <name> -n <namespace>
```
**Describe the solution you'd like**

We want to be able to push non-JSON secrets into HashiCorp vault. That is, given a Secret like:

```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: source-secret

steps.title: Apply the fix for PushSecrets should implement the same templating options as
steps.description:
## Problem Statement

It is not currently possible to use a PushSecret to push a whole Secret resource to a secret store. Since the `secretKey` field is mandatory, only a subset of the secret can be pushed.

## Related Issue

Related to #2166

## Proposed Changes

This MR introduce the possibility t
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: source-secret
  namespace: default
stringData:
  source-key: "this is a secret"
```

steps.title: Confirm PushSecrets should implement the same templating o is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: ## Problem Statement

It is not currently possible to use a PushSecret to push a whole Secret resource to a secret store. Since the `secretKey` field is mandatory, only a subset of the secret can be pushed.

## Related Issue

Related to #2166

## Proposed Changes

This MR introduce the possibility to not provide a `secretKey`, in this case the Secret resource's data field is converted to a JSON st. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: source-secret
  namespace: default
stringData:
  source-key: "this is a secret"

apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1alpha1
kind: PushSecret
metadata:
  name: pushsecret-example
  namespace: default
spec:
  refreshInterval: 10s
  secretStoreRefs:
    - name: vault-secretstore
      kind: SecretStore
  selector:
    secret:
      name: source-secret # Source Kubernetes secret to be pushed
  data:
    - secretKey: key1
    - secretKey: key2
  remoteRef: vault/secret
  template:
    engineVersion: v2
    data:
      token: {{ key1.token }}
      color: {{ key2.color | replace "red" "blue" }}

apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1alpha1
kind: PushSecret
metadata:
  name: pushsecret-example
spec:
  # ...
  template:
    # how can we support nested data structures?
    # how do we handle serialization?
    data:
      token: {{ key1.token }}
      color: {{ key2.color | replace "red" "blue" }}

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with external-secrets installed or the issue environment reproducible.
